goals: Expose HTTP endpoints for submitting all five inference operations (create image/video/audio, edit image, upscale image).
Provide a job status endpoint for polling.
Provide an SSE endpoint per job for real-time progress streaming without polling.
Provide job list and cancel endpoints for job management.

userStories.title: Submit inference jobs via REST
userStories.description: As an external consumer (web UI, script, webhook), I want to POST a job request and receive a job ID immediately so that I can track a long-running inference without keeping the HTTP connection open.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `POST /jobs/create/image` accepts a JSON body matching `GenerateImageRequest` from `@parallax/sdk` plus a required `model: string` field.
`POST /jobs/create/video` accepts a JSON body matching `GenerateVideoRequest` plus `model: string` and optional `input: string` (image path for i2v).
`POST /jobs/create/audio` accepts a JSON body matching `GenerateAudioRequest` plus `model: string`.
`POST /jobs/edit/image` accepts a JSON body matching `EditImageRequest` plus `model: string`.
`POST /jobs/upscale/image` accepts `{ image_path: string, model: string, output?: string }`.
All five endpoints call `submitJob()` from `@parallax/sdk/submit` and return `{ job_id: string, status: "queued" }` within 200ms.
Returns `400` with a descriptive error body when required fields are missing.
Visually verified: `curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/jobs/create/image -d '{"model":"sdxl","prompt":"a cat"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'` returns `{ job_id, status: "queued" }`.

userStories.title: Get job status by ID
userStories.description: As a consumer, I want to `GET /jobs/:id` and receive the current state of a job so that I can poll status without an open SSE connection.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `GET /jobs/:id` returns a JSON object with: `id`, `status` (`waiting|active|completed|failed`), `progress` (0â€“100 number), `output` (string or null), `error` (string or null), `createdAt` (epoch ms), `startedAt` (epoch ms or null), `finishedAt` (epoch ms or null).
Returns `404` with `{ error: "Job not found" }` when the ID does not exist.
`bun typecheck` passes.

userStories.title: Stream job progress via SSE
userStories.description: As a consumer, I want to `GET /jobs/:id/stream` and receive Server-Sent Events until the job completes or fails so that I get real-time progress without polling.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `GET /jobs/:id/stream` returns a `text/event-stream` response.
While the job is active or waiting, emits `event: progress\ndata: {"pct":N,"step":"..."}` every 500ms.
When the job completes, emits `event: completed\ndata: {"output":"<path>"}` then closes the stream.
When the job fails, emits `event: failed\ndata: {"error":"<reason>"}` then closes the stream.
Returns `404` before opening the stream if the job ID does not exist.
`@elysiajs/stream` is added as a dependency.
Visually verified in browser: EventSource connects, receives progress events, stream closes on completion.

userStories.title: List jobs
userStories.description: As a consumer, I want to `GET /jobs` and see all recent jobs with their status so that I can inspect the queue state.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `GET /jobs` returns `{ jobs: JobSummary[], counts: { waiting, active, completed, failed } }`.
`JobSummary` includes: `id`, `status`, `progress`, `model`, `action`, `media`, `createdAt`.
Defaults to returning at most 50 most-recent jobs.
Supports optional query param `?status=active|completed|failed|waiting` to filter.
`bun typecheck` passes.

userStories.title: Cancel a job
userStories.description: As a consumer, I want to `DELETE /jobs/:id` to cancel a running or waiting job so that I can abort inference that is no longer needed.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `DELETE /jobs/:id` calls `getQueue().cancel(jobId)` and returns `{ cancelled: true }`.
Returns `404` if the job ID does not exist.
Returns `409` with `{ error: "Job already completed" }` if the job is already in a terminal state.
`bun typecheck` passes.

userStories.title: Health endpoint enrichment
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `GET /health` to include queue statistics so that I can verify the queue is operational.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `GET /health` returns `{ status: "ok", queue: { waiting, active, completed, failed } }`.
Does not break existing behavior (still returns HTTP 200).
`bun typecheck` passes.

functionalRequirements.description: All route handlers must use Elysia's native TypeScript input validation (no manual `if (!body.field)` checks).
The server listens on port `3000` by default, overridable via `PORT` environment variable.
CORS must be enabled for all origins in development (`@elysiajs/cors`).
Unhandled errors in route handlers must return `500` with `{ error: "Internal server error" }` â€” not crash the server.
`parallax_ms/package.json` must declare `@parallax/sdk` as `workspace:*` dependency if not already present.

All job submission endpoints must resolve the correct Python script path using the same `getScript()` / model registry logic already in `parallax_cli/src/models/registry.ts` â€” do not hardcode script paths.
